what: Plot a consensus rank-selection (nmfkc.consensus) object — plot.nmfkc.consensus
title: Plot a consensus rank-selection (nmfkc.consensus) object — plot.nmfkc.consensus
text: Two views nmfkc.consensus result: type = "criteria" (default): stability curves cophenetic (blue) dispersion (red) rank. "best" marker drawn – stable rank tends coarse one, curves shown inspection rather optimum. type = "heatmap": consensus matrix requested rank, reordered average-linkage hierarchical clustering \(1 - \bar C\) (blue = 0 / different cluster, red = 1 / cluster).  Requires keep.consensus = TRUE compute time.

title: Plot a consensus rank-selection (nmfkc.consensus) object — plot.nmfkc.consensus
code: # S3 method for class 'nmfkc.consensus' plot(   x,   type = c("criteria", "heatmap"),   rank = NULL,   mfrow = NULL,   mar = NULL,   col = grDevices::hcl.colors(50, "Blue-Red"),   main = NULL,   ... )

title: Plot a consensus rank-selection (nmfkc.consensus) object — plot.nmfkc.consensus
text: x object class "nmfkc.consensus". type "criteria" "heatmap". rank type = "heatmap", rank(s) display. NULL (default) shows every rank stored x. mfrow Panel layout multiple heatmaps, c(nrow, ncol).  NULL (default) uses n2mfrow near-square grid. mar Per-panel margins c(b, l, t, r) heatmap view.  NULL (default) uses tight margins grid wider margins (sample labels) single rank shown. col Heatmap colour palette (length-50 blue--red default). main Plot title "criteria" view (heatmap panels titled per rank). ... arguments passed underlying plot / image.

title: Plot convergence diagnostics for NMF models — plot.nmfre
text: x fitted model object. ... Additional graphical arguments passed plot. plot.nmf.sem: objective plot. One "full" (default; loss + penalties, actual monotonically-decreasing quantity multiplicative updates minimize), "reconstruction" (Frobenius distance , \(\| Y_1 - X B \|_F^2\)), "" (overlay legend).  "" useful diagnosing whether regularization actively shaping solution: two curves diverge, penalties pulling optimizer away pure least-squares minimum.

what: Predict method for nmfae objects — predict.nmfae
title: Predict method for nmfae objects — predict.nmfae
text: predict.nmfae computes fitted predicted values three-layer NMF model. Without newY2, returns -sample fitted values \(X_1 \Theta X_2 Y_2\). newY2, computes --sample predictions \(X_1 \Theta X_2 \cdot \mathrm{newY2}\). type = "class", column classified row maximum predicted value (useful \(Y_1\) one-hot class matrix nmfkc.class). Y1 (actual values) provided, stored attribute plot.predict.nmfae can produce observed-vs-predicted scatter plot (type = "response") confusion matrix heatmap (type = "class").

title: Predict method for nmfae objects — predict.nmfae
code: # S3 method for class 'nmfae' predict(object, newY2 = NULL, Y1 = NULL, type = c("response", "class"), ...)

title: Predict method for nmfae objects — predict.nmfae
text: object object class "nmfae" returned nmfae. newY2 Optional new input matrix (P2 x M) prediction. NULL, returns -sample fitted values. Y1 Optional actual output matrix comparison plotting. type Character. "response" (default) returns predicted matrix. "class" returns factor predicted class labels (row max value). ... used.

title: Predict method for nmfae objects — predict.nmfae
text: type = "response": matrix class "predict.nmfae". type = "class": factor class "predict.nmfae" predicted class labels. Y1 provided, actual classes stored attr(result, "actual").

Covariate columns extracted automatically using stored formula metadata. newdata newA supplied, newdata takes precedence (warning). type Type prediction return. Error (Boot) z value   Pr(>z)  #> intercept:Basis1     0.014      6.427  3.254    0.00   0.4991   #>     speed:Basis1     2.851      0.456  0.432    6.25 2.118e-10 *** #> --- #> Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1

title: nmfkc 0.8.2
text: layout choices reordered recommendation (neato, fdp, twopi, circo, dot), default changes "fdp" "neato", separates community graphs clearly. Raising threshold (e.g. 0.2–0.3) declutters weak membership edges.

what: Bug fix: nmfkc.net.DOT() mis-detected type = "bi" as "tri"
title: nmfkc 0.8.2
text: bi-vs-tri auto-detection ignored result’s $type field fell back .equal(C, diag(Q)), fails C carries dimnames (reports names mismatch). type = "bi" fit therefore treated "tri", drawing inter-class interaction layer bi model () . Detection now uses $type first (falling back dimnames-safe identity check), "bi" correctly draws inter-class edges.

title: nmfkc 0.8.2
text: Fine-tuning arguments move ... (safe defaults): nmfkc.bicv() now nmfkc.bicv(Y, rank, ...) (nfolds = 2 per Owen & Perry, plus seed, nnls.maxit, via ...), nmfkc.consensus() nmfkc.consensus(Y, , rank, nrun, keep.consensus, ...) (seed, pac.range via ...). Existing named-argument calls unaffected.

title: nmfkc 0.8.2
text: signature trimmed essentials nmfkc.ard(Y, rank, nrun, plot, ...); everything else (prior, seed, , b, maxit, epsilon, tol) moves ... safe defaults, typical call just nmfkc.ard(Y, rank = K). nrun now defaults 10 (1): ARD sensitive point estimate, several restarts give stable modal rank default. help now states explicitly implementation Euclidean () case Tan & Fevotte (2013) default b empirical energy scale, paper’s method--moments value (Eq. 38).

title: nmfkc 0.8.2
text: default b now initial per-component energy scale (nrow(Y) + ncol(Y)) / K * mean(Y) instead fixed 0.001 * mean(Y). old fixed fraction -pruned (winner-take-collapse onto one dominant component) (F + N)/K large; new scale-aware default recovers genuine low-rank structure stably (e.g. clean rank-3 signal: relevance 1, 0.99, 0.87, 0, ..., restarts agree).

what: New nmfkc.ard(): ARD rank determination (Tan & Fevotte 2013, prototype)
title: nmfkc 0.8.2
text: Automatic Relevance Determination NMF rank (Euclidean). Fits NMF -complete rank prunes automatically: component carries relevance weight inverse-gamma prior multiplicative updates gain penalty (L2 half-normal / L1 exponential) drives unsupported components zero. number surviving components estimated rank – rank scan. Returns "nmfkc.ard" object print relevance-bar plot. Plain NMF ; sensitive point estimate (depends prior / start / init), complement CV / consensus engines, sole criterion.

title: nmfkc 0.8.2
text: bioinformatics-standard stability approach, lightweight engine like nmfkc.ecv / nmfkc.bicv. rank runs NMF nrun times random initializations (X.init = "runif"), builds consensus matrix per-run hard clusterings, returns two stability scores per rank: cophenetic (cophenetic correlation coefficient, Brunet et al. 2004) dispersion (Kim & Park 2007, [0,1]). Unlike CV engines, good rank maximizes stability. Optional keep.consensus = TRUE returns consensus matrices. Also reports pac, Proportion Ambiguous Clustering (Senbabaoglu et al. 2014; fraction consensus entries ambiguous interval pac.range, default (0.1, 0.9)). Lower better sensitive often-saturated cophenetic. print/criteria-plot show three metrics. Returns "nmfkc.consensus" object print plot methods: plot(cs) (type = "criteria") draws stability curves; plot(cs, type = "heatmap", rank = ...) draws consensus matrix heatmap(s) reordered hierarchical clustering (default = ranks n2mfrow grid; mfrow overridable).

what: New nmfkc.bicv(): bi-cross-validation for rank selection
title: nmfkc 0.8.2
text: Owen & Perry’s (2009) bi-cross-validation (BCV), lightweight CV engine spirit nmfkc.ecv: returns held-error per rank (objfunc, sigma) nothing . Holds row-block column-block , fits NMF retained block, predicts held-block folding held-rows/columns onto fixed factors via non-negative regression (information leakage, unlike element-wise nmfkc.ecv). nfolds = 2 (leave half rows / half columns) per Owen & Perry’s recommendation.

what: *.rank: eff.rank.idx shown for context (no best marker)
title: nmfkc 0.8.2
text: broken-stick-corrected effective-rank index (eff.rank.idx, green) drawn context longer carries “Best (Max)” marker: factor-utilization diagnostic (even relative random null), predictive rank optimum. recommended rank driven solely ECV minimum R-squared elbow.

title: nmfkc 0.8.2
text: *.rank criteria table gains effective.rank.expected (broken-stick / uniform-Dirichlet null exp(H_Q - 1), H_Q = Q-th harmonic number) effective.rank.index, [0, 1] index (effective.rank - expected) / (Q - expected) (clamped). index anchors 0 random null 1 perfect evenness, removing small-rank inflation raw effective.rank / Q. maximum meaningful rank, diagnostics plot now draws corrected index (green, eff.rank.idx) restored “Best (Max)” marker place raw ratio.

title: nmfkc 0.8.2
text: rank-selection functions (nmfkc.rank(), nmfkc.net.rank(), nmfkc.signed.rank(), nmfae.rank(), nmfae.signed.rank()) now return classed object ("nmf.rank"). plot() redraws three-criterion diagnostics plot (honouring main, xlab, ylab, lwd) print() shows recommended rank, per-criterion best ranks, criteria table. constructor draws immediately plot = TRUE; $rank.best $criteria fields unchanged, existing code keeps working.

what: New nmf.cluster.flow(): cluster-flow diagram across ranks
title: nmfkc 0.8.2
text: nmf.cluster.flow() nmf.cluster.criteria() now treat supplied fits generic  (kept given order,  sorted rank), rank fitted different models also supported. gain names argument x-axis tick labels (default: result’s $rank), nmf.cluster.flow() reference argument now  (1-based position) result defines colours – rank value – defaulting central result floor(length(fits) / 2) + 1 (e.g. 2nd 2 3 results). adjusted Rand index (ARI) pair adjacent ranks now computed printed along top figure (returned $ARI, length ), summarizing much hard clustering changes one rank next. cluster box now tinted  reference colour among individuals contains (colour shared member lines); ties broken favour earliest palette entry (smallest reference-cluster id). shows glance reference cluster dominates box rank. nmf.cluster.flow() now inserts gap one average cluster () clusters per-rank layout sizes grey box exactly minimum/maximum position members, cluster boxes clearly separated gaps maximized. rank normalized full height independently. cluster number dominant-factor index (argmax coefficient) fit, kept -matches factor/basis numbering supplied models. factor never dominates individual leaves empty, unused cluster number (gap, e.g. labels 2, 3 1) – correct consistent fit, labels renumbered. nmf.cluster.flow() now returns classed object dedicated plot() method, diagram can (re)drawn plot(fl, col = , lwd = , xlab = , ylab = , main = ) – colour vector (indexed reference cluster), line width, axis labels title honoured. constructor still draws immediately default (plot = TRUE) forwards graphical arguments plot method; use plot = FALSE build object plot later. print() method shows adjacent-rank ARI full  cluster table. nmf.cluster.flow(fits, reference = ) takes list models fitted different ranks (non-negative MU family) draws alluvial / Sankey-style diagram hard sample clustering changes rank : individual flows left--right across ranks (x-axis), vertical position set cluster (clusters reordered per rank barycenter heuristic reduce crossings), lines coloured cluster  rank – one can watch reference clusters split merge. every rank translucent grey box drawn  cluster’s members cluster number centred inside, grouping labels visible ranks (reference). default line palette now strong, well-separated qualitative set (ColorBrewer , pale colours) can overridden . Returns (invisibly)  table rows = individuals, columns = rank, entries = cluster number.

what: New nmf.cluster.criteria(): sample-clustering quality across ranks
title: nmfkc 0.8.2
text: nmf.cluster.criteria(fits, Y) takes  (one per rank; single fit also accepted) reports clustering-quality criteria silhouette, CPCC, dist.cor rank, returning per-rank $criteria table (mirroring nmf.cluster.flow()). plot() (line plot three criteria vs rank) print() (table) methods, draws immediately plot = TRUE. Works family (nmfkc, nmfkc.signed, nmfae, nmfae.signed, nmfkc.net, nmfre, nmf.sem/nmf.ffb; last needs exogenous block via Y2). clustering-stability diagnostics, deliberately separate rank-selection *.rank functions (r.squared / effective rank / ECV). Hard sample clustering needs non-negative coefficient/score matrix (valid membership simplex). nmf.cluster.criteria() detects actual coefficient: non-negative hard-label silhouette (cluster sizes) returned; signed silhouette NA distance-based CPCC dist.cor still computed. (ARI reported – compares two clusterings, e.g. across ranks resamples, single-fit quantity.) nmfkc.rank() longer carries ARI, silhouette, CPCC, dist.cor criteria table – clustering-stability metrics now live nmf.cluster.criteria(). five *.rank functions return five columns (rank, effective.rank, effective.rank.ratio, r.squared, sigma.ecv). Per-rank fits use detail = "fast", expensive O(N^2) distance computations skipped rank selection. rank.best unchanged. *.rank functions now emit one-line message pointing nmf.cluster.criteria() clustering quality.

what: Rank-selection functions for the other NMF families
title: nmfkc 0.8.2
text: New nmfkc.net.rank(), nmfkc.signed.rank(), nmfae.rank() (paired ) nmfae.signed.rank() (paired) bring nmfkc.rank-style rank selection multiplicative-update models. reports three criteria well defined every family – r.squared, effective rank (utilization), element-wise CV error sigma.ecv – returns list(rank.best, criteria). (nmf.ffb / nmfre covered: support element masking ECV needs.) nmfkc.rank() plot simplified unified. *.rank functions now share one back-end .rank.finish() draw concise three-criterion figure: r.squared (red), eff.rank (green), sigma.ecv (blue, right axis), line points, rank-number labels, highlighted best marker – “Best (Elbow)” R-squared knee, “Best (Peak)” effective-rank utilization, “Best (Min)” CV minimum. nmfkc.rank() still computes ARI, silhouette, CPCC, dist.cor criteria table, longer plots . four new *.rank functions gain detail argument matching nmfkc.rank: "full" (default) runs element-wise CV reports sigma.ecv; "fast" skips (expensive) CV, plot shows r.squared eff.rank recommended rank falls back R-squared elbow.

title: nmfkc 0.8.2
text: four element-wise cross-validation functions (nmfkc.ecv(), nmfae.ecv(), nmfkc.signed.ecv(), nmfae.signed.ecv()) now build folds single internal helper .ecv.make.folds(), removing four near-identical copies fold-partitioning loop. nmfkc.net.ecv() keeps symmetric upper-triangle folds. element-wise CV functions now share one config-indexed loop driver .ecv.run(labels, nfolds, run_one,   progress): single-rank ones (nmfkc.ecv(), nmfkc.net.ecv(), nmfkc.signed.ecv()) -grid ones (nmfae.ecv(), nmfae.signed.ecv()). supplies model-specific run_one(, k) closure (mask fold, refit config , return held-loss) optional progress callback; .ecv.run() handles config--fold loop, objfunc/sigma/objfunc.fold aggregation, naming. removes last copies CV-loop machinery, including per-grid reshaping nmfae.ecv(). refactor behaviour-preserving: seed folds CV values (objfunc, sigma, objfunc.fold, names/labels) byte--byte identical , verified across EU KL losses, symmetric (upper-triangle) case, paired full  grids.

title: nmfkc 0.8.2
text: New shared internal helpers .print.fit.statistics() .print.structure.diagnostics() render “Statistics” / “Goodness fit” “Structure Diagnostics” blocks summary.nmfkc(), summary.nmfae(), summary.nmfkc.net() (incl. signed variant). Labels padded common width values column-aligned, fields absent given model skipped automatically (e.g. nmfkc.net residual SE), future fit statistic sparsity row now added one place instead per-summary.

what: Effective Rank in all five MU-family summaries
title: nmfkc 0.8.2
text: summary() now reports Effective Rank x.xx / Q  (NN.N%) – absolute value, nominal rank, utilization ratio effective.rank / Q percentage – nmfkc(), nmfkc.net(), nmfae(), nmf.ffb() / nmf.sem(), nmfre() — previously nmfkc() showed . computed new shared internal helper .effective.rank(B) model’s natural  coefficient/score matrix: coefficients  (nmfkc), latent encoding  (nmfae), node membership  (nmfkc.net), latent scores  (nmf.ffb), BLUP scores  (nmfre). NA .

what: Rank-selection diagnostics: silhouette / CPCC fixed, IC removed
title: nmfkc 0.8.2
text: silhouette now computed original data space. used evaluated rank- B.prob simplex, whose dimension changes ; made monotone  (always favouring smallest rank) hid genuine cluster structure. now standard mean silhouette width dist(t(Y)) (fixed original-data sample distances) per-sample hard labels — k-means convention. data real clusters now shows interior optimum (e.g. road-OD network peaks rank cross-validation minimum). CPCC now classic cophenetic correlation dist(t(B)). used computed soft co-membership t(B.prob) %*%   B.prob, nearly flat across . now cor(dist(t(B)), cophenetic(hclust(dist(t(B))))) — well hierarchical clustering rank- coefficient distances reproduces distances (Sokal & Rohlf). now varies  recovers interior optimum. Removed ICp, AIC, BIC nmfkc()’s criterion list, summary.nmfkc(), nmfkc.rank()’s table. Empirically (across three real datasets) ICp monotone increasing (always selecting ) AIC monotone decreasing (always selecting largest ); NMF, parameter count grows , information criteria usable interior optimum, misleading rather informative. internal helper .silhouette.simple() (centroid-approximate, took B.prob matrix) replaced .silhouette.mean(D, labels), returns exact mean silhouette width distance matrix labels.

what: Breaking change: symmetric NMF removed from nmfkc()
title: nmfkc 0.8.2
text: Y.symmetric = "bi" / "tri" option (deprecated v0.7.x) removed nmfkc() nmfkc.ecv(). Symmetric NMF network data now lives exclusively dedicated nmfkc.net() / nmfkc.net.ecv() functions, use correct Frobenius bilateral-gradient updates. Passing Y.symmetric nmfkc() nmfkc.ecv() now stops message pointing replacement: nmfkc.net(Y, rank, type = "tri") (types "tri", "bi", "signed"). also removes bi/tri code branches (cube-root damping, fixed C = , tri C-update, upper-triangle CV folds) nmfkc(), simplifying core function.

title: nmfkc 0.8.2
text: nmfkc() now reports criterion$effective.rank, effective rank fit: exp Shannon entropy explained-variance distribution p_k = var(B[k, ]) / sum_j var(B[j, ]). trace identity sum_k var(B[k, ]) = tr(Cov(B)), p_k exact fraction total coefficient variance carried factor k, entropy genuine additive decomposition (variances add; standard deviations , variance — sd — natural partner entropy ). ranges [1, Q] counts many latent factors actively shape across-sample variation (dead, zero-variance factors drop ). PCA-style explained-variance / effective-dimensionality measure reuses exp(entropy) functional form Roy & Vetterli (2007). summary.nmfkc() prints Effective Rank: x.xx / Q. nmfkc.rank() adds effective.rank column criteria table. effective rank plateaus well nominal rank, extra factors carrying additional coefficient variance — signal rank -specified. nmfkc.rank(plot = TRUE) overlays eff.rank curve (effective rank divided nominal rank, [0, 1], solid green line) diagnostics plot. peak utilization curve marks rank latent factors carry evenly distributed variance.

title: nmfkc 0.8.2
text: Removed B.prob.sd.min B.prob.entropy.mean nmfkc()’s criterion list, summary.nmfkc(), nmfkc.rank()’s criteria table plot. three B.prob.* peakedness metrics monotone rank Q, carry peak/elbow signal rank selection (verified empirically); principled rank signals ECV, R-squared elbow, new effective.rank utilization. B.prob.max.mean (clustering crispness) retained, summary.nmfkc() (“Clustering Crispness”) criterion list. fixed Q remains useful confidence check — mean dominant-cluster membership — treating B.cluster hard labels. longer shown nmfkc.rank() (cross-Q), 1/Q baseline shift makes misleading. summary.nmfkc() longer prints “Clustering Entropy” (duplicated crispness information).

title: nmfkc 0.8.2
text: r.squared: Pearson  (scale-invariant, ). Unchanged . r.squared.uncentered: . Baseline = zero matrix (natural non-negative factorizations without intercept); matches “uncentered R²” intercept-free regression. r.squared.centered: . Baseline = per-row mean; standard (“centered”) multivariate- regression ; equals 0 model predicts row mean.

title: nmfkc 0.8.2
text: nmfkc.net(): r.squared now correctly excludes weight-zero (NA-masked) entries Y.weights supplied auto-masking effect, matching convention used nmfkc(), nmfae(), nmfae.signed(), nmfkc.signed(). Previously correlation computed full matrix including replaced-NA cells, giving distorted r.squared.

title: nmfkc 0.8.2
text: nmfkc(): removed Examples 3 & 4 (deprecated Y.symmetric = "bi"/"tri"); documentation now points users \link{nmfkc.net}() symmetric NMF. summary.nmf.sem(): example code, @param, @seealso updated use canonical nmf.ffb name (S3 method continues dispatch correctly via c("nmf.ffb", "nmf.sem") inheritance).

title: nmfkc 0.7.3
text: CRAN release: 2026-05-13

title: nmfkc 0.7.3
text: README nmf-sem--nmfkc.Rmd vignette code now reference canonical nmf.ffb.* aliases (nmf.ffb(), nmf.ffb.cv(), nmf.ffb.DOT()) instead legacy nmf.sem.* names. names continue work; change affects users see GitHub Pages homepage vignette source.

what: Headline: NMF-FFB rebrand and full bootstrap inference
title: nmfkc 0.7.2
text: nmf.ffb* family added canonical alias nmf.sem* (Satoh 2025, arXiv:2512.18250 adopts “NMF-FFB” — Non-negative Matrix Factorization Feed-Forward + Feedback — model’s canonical name). nmf.sem* continues work shares return classes (c("nmf.ffb", "nmf.sem") c("nmf.ffb.inference", "nmf.sem.inference", ...)), existing scripts unaffected. nmf.sem.inference() / nmf.ffb.inference(): replaced legacy 1-step Newton wild bootstrap full X-fixed pair bootstrap. Resamples columns (Y1, Y2), refits (C1, C2) X held original fit, reports per-element support_rate = mean(|c_b| >   threshold) together percentile CIs. Significance markers (* / ** / *** sup > 0.95 / 0.99 / 0.999) follow lavaan convention. Theta_1 (feedback) Theta_2 (exogenous) inference targets (previous version covered Theta_2). nmf.sem() / nmf.ffb(): now runs nmfkc(Y1, = Y2) internally default X.init string method, forwarding X.init, X.L2.ortho, epsilon, maxit, seed. feedforward fit used X warm-start baseline SC.map. nmfkc.baseline = FALSE opts .

title: nmfkc 0.7.2
text: nmf.sem.inference(): fixed dimension bug Leontief identity matrix (I_mat <- diag(Q) diag(P1)); previously every replicate silently marked invalid P1 != Q. nmfkc.net(): now auto-masks NA entries Y (parity four NMF variants); previously errored min(Y) < 0 check Y contained NA. nmfkc(): Fixed C matrix asymmetry tri-symmetric NMF (Y.symmetric = "tri"). C update using stale B XB computed old X; now B XB recomputed X updated. Also fixed column reordering permute rows columns C. Previously relative asymmetry reach ~46%; now machine precision (~1e-14).

title: nmfkc 0.7.2
text: Y.weights semantics unified lm()-style weighted least squares across nmfkc(), nmfae(), nmfkc.net(), nmfkc.signed(), nmfae.signed(): loss now sum(W * (Y - Yhat)^2) (linear W, matching lm()’s weights argument). Binary masks (W ∈ {0, 1}; standard ECV / NA-mask case) unaffected since W = W^2. MU functions now emit "maximum iterations (N) reached..." warning maxit exhausted without meeting relative- tolerance criterion (previously silent nmfae, nmfae.signed, nmfkc.net, nmfkc.signed, nmfre, nmf.sem). MU functions now share maxit = 5000 default (5000 / 20000 / 50000 inconsistently). Together maxit warning , users see explicit feedback 5000 insufficient can opt larger cap. New shared internal helper .init_X_method() X initialization via "nndsvd" / "kmeans" / "kmeansar" / "runif" / numeric matrix. NMF families now use dispatch logic; previous ad-hoc inline implementations removed. nmf.sem() returns SC.map (input-output structural fidelity: correlation equilibrium operator feedforward baseline mapping; Satoh 2025 §4.SC.map) automatically nmfkc.baseline supplied computed internally. summary.nmf.sem(): rewritten display full-bootstrap inference output — separate Theta_1 / Theta_2 blocks Estimate | CI_low | CI_high | support | Pr(>0) | sig, plus bootstrap meta-info header. coef.nmf.sem(): now returns long-format data frame rows every entry C1 C2 (Type | Basis | Covariate |   Estimate); previously returned C2 matrix inference run. Schema matches inference-augmented output uniformity. plot.nmf.sem(): default trace now objfunc.full (loss + penalties — actual monotonically-decreasing quantity multiplicative updates minimize) instead objfunc (reconstruction ). New argument = "full" | "reconstruction" | "". nmf.sem.DOT(): significance stars now appear Theta_1 (feedback Y1 → F) edges addition Theta_2 (exogenous Y2 → F); X (F → Y1) edges remain unstarred since basis inference target. plot.nmfae.ecv(): Heatmap cell text color now always black better readability light-colored cells. nmfkc(): X.init = "runif" now supports nstart > 1 multi-start initialization. Multiple random starting points evaluated 10 standard NMF iterations, best (lowest Frobenius error) selected. nmfae(), nmfre(): r.squared now computed cor(Y, fitted)^2 (squared correlation observed fitted values), consistent nmfkc(). Previously nmfae() used 1 - SS_res/SS_tot nmfre() used regression-style R-squared, can behave unexpectedly intercept-free non-negative models. nmfkc.kernel.beta.nearest.med(): added candidates argument controlling bandwidth grid. Options: "7points" (new default, t = {-1,-2/3,-1/3,0,1/3,2/3,1}), "4points" (t = {-1/2, 0, 1/2, 1}), user-supplied numeric vector  values. Previously grid silently differed -landmark (Uk = NULL; 4 points) landmark (7 points) branches.

title: nmfkc 0.7.2
text: nmfkc.signed(): NMF-KC signed covariate/coefficient. Model  ,  (signed),  real-valued. Uses Ding et al. (2010) sign-splitting + Direct MU;  may also contain negative entries (semi-NMF regression). Supports Y.weights element-wise masking. nmfkc.signed.cv(), nmfkc.signed.ecv(): column-wise element-wise k-fold CV rank selection signed data. nmfae.signed(): Three-layer autoencoder  .  preserve soft clustering decoder encoder sides bottleneck  can carry negative weights (e.g., anti-correlated properties). Hybrid warm-start (nmfae()) + Direct MU multi-restart. nmfae.signed.ecv(): element-wise CV (decoder-rank, encoder-rank) selection. nmfae.signed.inference(): sandwich SE + wild bootstrap  (non-negativity projection  since signed). S3 methods predict.*.signed(), plot.*.signed(), summary.*.signed(), nmfae.signed.rename() helper.

title: nmfkc 0.7.2
text: nmfkc.net(): Single unified entry point symmetric NMF network data, type = "tri" | "bi" | "signed". three variants use Frobenius-full bilateral gradient (supersedes one-sided approximation nmfkc(Y.symmetric = ...)). type = "signed" supports signed  via Ding et al. (2010) sign-splitting, preserving  soft clustering allowing inter-cluster repulsion. returned object’s fields uniform across types:    tri/bi, populated matrices signed.  always populated (identity bi, non-negative tri, signed signed). nmfkc.net.ecv(): Element-wise cross-validation upper-triangle folds (mirrored lower triangle prevent symmetry leakage). Unified entry point type = "tri" | "bi" | "signed" (calls nmfkc.net() matching type fold). nmfkc.net.DOT(): Graphviz DOT visualization symmetric NMF networks. Displays basis--node membership edges inter-basis interaction edges (C matrix) significance stars. Now signed parameter (auto-detected class) render negative C entries dashed edges. nmfkc.net.inference(): Statistical inference symmetric NMF. Wrapper around nmfkc.inference() = t(X). Returns -diagonal C coefficients sandwich SE wild bootstrap.

title: nmfkc 0.7.2
text: nmfkc(Y, Y.symmetric = "bi"|"tri"): Deprecated favor nmfkc.net(Y, type = "bi"|"tri"). old implementation uses one-sided gradient approximation empirically converges  theoretically incorrect extend signed . deprecated branch still works v0.6.8 (deprecation warning) removed future release.

what: Parameter Renames (old names remain usable for backward compatibility)
title: nmfkc 0.7.2
text: nmf.sem.DOT(): weight_scale_y2f → weight_scale_c2, weight_scale_fy1 → weight_scale_x1 (matrix-name-based naming, consistent nmfae.DOT() nmfkc.DOT()). nmf.sem.DOT(): sig.level moved threshold consistency .DOT functions.

title: nmfkc 0.7.2
text: README, vignettes, roxygen @title / @description updated use NMF-FFB canonical model name (“(formerly NMF-SEM)” attached first mention discoverability legacy term). File names (R/nmf.sem.R, vignettes/nmf-sem--   nmfkc.Rmd, man/nmf.sem.Rd), function names (nmf.sem*), S3 classes ("nmf.sem") unchanged URLs existing scripts continue work.

title: nmfkc 0.6.7
text: CRAN release: 2026-04-15

title: nmfkc 0.6.7
text: Added fitted.nmfae() residuals.nmfae() S3 methods; previously fitted() nmfae object silently returned NULL wrong field name ($XB instead $Y1hat) used.

what: Naming Unification (old names remain usable for backward compatibility)
title: nmfkc 0.6.7
text: Coefficient tables: inference functions now use Basis / Covariate columns (Factor/Exogenous nmf.sem.inference(), Decoder/Encoder nmfae.inference()). Wild bootstrap defaults unified: wild.B = 500, wild.seed = 123 across inference functions. First argument .DOT functions renamed result consistency. CV tuning parameters (nfolds, seed, shuffle) moved ... nmfkc.ecv(), nmfae.ecv(), nmfae.cv(), nmf.sem.cv(); div also accepted backward compatibility.

title: nmfkc 0.6.6
text: nmfkc.criterion(): Extracted criterion computation nmfkc() standalone exported function. Supports detail = "full" / "fast" / "minimal" control computation cost. nmfre.inference(): Separated statistical inference nmfre() optimization. Returns coefficient table SE, z-values, p-values via wild bootstrap. nmf.sem.inference(): Statistical inference C2 parameter matrix NMF-SEM. Uses sandwich SE wild bootstrap. S3 methods coef(), fitted(), residuals() model classes (nmfkc, nmfae, nmfre, nmf.sem). S3 methods plot() nmfre nmf.sem (convergence diagnostics). summary.nmf.sem(): Stability diagnostics, fit statistics, C2 coefficient table.

what: Parameter Renames (old names remain usable for backward compatibility)

text: nmfkc(), nmfkc.rank(): save.time / save.memory → detail nmfae(): Q → rank, R → rank.encoder nmfre(): Q → rank, dfU.cap.rate → df.rate nmfre.dfU.scan(), nmfkc.ar.degree.cv(): Q → rank nmfkc.residual.plot(): Y_XB_palette → fitted.palette, E_palette → residual.palette nmfkc.kernel.beta.nearest.med(): block_size → block.size, sample_size → sample.size

title: nmfkc 0.6.6
text: hide.isolated option added .DOT functions (default TRUE). nmf.sem.DOT(): Added sig.level parameter; C2 edges decorated significance stars. nmfkc(): Added X.restriction = "none" option X.init = "kmeansar" initialization. Added arXiv/DOI references roxygen documentation main functions. @section Lifecycle: Experimental added nmfae(). Removed mc.cores parallel option nmfae.ecv() CRAN compliance.

title: nmfkc 0.6.0
text: Fixed variable T shadowing TRUE information criterion computation. Fixed nmfkc.ecv() use KL divergence evaluation method="KL". Added performance flags (save.time=TRUE) nmfkc.ecv() inner calls. Fixed zero-division nmfkc.rank() elbow normalization R-squared values identical. Fixed parameter name mismatch (rank → Q) nmfkc.rank() call nmfkc.ecv(). Fixed descending loop nmf.sem.split() P=2. Added input validation n.exogenous nmf.sem.split().

title: nmfkc 0.6.0
text: Added roxygen documentation summary.nmfkc() print.summary.nmfkc(). Added @return plot.nmfkc() predict.nmfkc(). Added missing @return items (method, n.missing, n.total, rank, mae) nmfkc().

title: nmfkc 0.6.0
text: Replaced T/F TRUE/FALSE. Replaced 1:length() seq_along(). Changed default font Meiryo Arial DOT functions. Aligned nmf.sem.cv() defaults nmf.sem().

what: Graphviz DOT Output Consolidation and Cleanup
title: nmfkc 0.5.8
text: Harmonized DOT-generating functions (nmf.sem.DOT, nmfkc.DOT, nmfkc.ar.DOT) consistent structure, naming conventions, visualization logic. Standardized node edge formatting rules, including unified cluster behavior, color schemes, edge-scaling conventions. Implemented threshold-aware coefficient labeling displayed numerical precision aligns visualization threshold, preventing misleadingly detailed labels. Removed unused redundant DOT fragments improved compatibility across Graphviz engines. Enhanced layout readability consistent indentation, node grouping, suppression isolated nodes specific visualization modes (e.g., type = "YA" nmfkc.DOT). Refactored expanded internal DOT helper functions (.nmfkc_dot_format_coef, .nmfkc_dot_digits_from_threshold, .nmfkc_dot_cluster_nodes, etc.) better maintainability uniform behavior. New Function: Implemented nmfkc.ecv() Element-wise Cross-Validation (Wold’s CV). function randomly masks elements observation matrix evaluate structural reconstruction error. provides statistically robust criterion rank selection, avoiding monotonic error decrease often seen standard column-wise CV. Supports vector input rank evaluate multiple ranks simultaneously. Missing Value & Weight Support: nmfkc() nmfkc.cv() now fully support missing values (NA) observation weights via hidden argument Y.weights (passed ...). Y contains NAs, automatically detected masked (assigned weight 0) optimization. Rank Selection Diagnostics (nmfkc.rank): Dual-Axis Visualization: plot now displays fitting metrics (\(R^2\), etc.) left axis ECV Sigma (RMSE) right axis (blue line). Elbow: Geometric elbow point \(R^2\) curve. Min: Minimum error point Element-wise CV. save.time defaults FALSE, enabling robust Element-wise CV calculation default. Argument Standardization: Unified rank argument name rank across functions (nmfkc, nmfkc.cv, nmfkc.ecv, nmfkc.rank). legacy argument Q still supported backward compatibility internally mapped rank. Summary Improvements: Sparsity Basis (\(X\)) Coefficients (\(B\)). Clustering Entropy (indicating “Crisp” vs “Ambiguous” clustering). Clustering Crispness (Mean Max Probability). Number percentage missing values \(Y\). Improvements: Added validation check nmfkc.ar() ensure input Y missing values (propagated covariate matrix VAR models). Refined nmfkc.residual.plot() layout margins better visibility titles. Updated documentation reflect changes. Regularization Update: regularization scheme revised L2 (ridge) L1 (lasso-type) penalties. gamma now controls L1 penalty coefficient matrix ( B = C ), promoting sparsity sample-wise coefficients. new argument lambda added control L1 penalty parameter matrix ( C ), encouraging sparsity shared template structure. parameters can passed ellipsis (...) nmfkc() related functions. Function Signature Simplification:** Many less-frequently used arguments nmfkc() (e.g., gamma, X.restriction, X.init) nmfkc.cv() (e.g., div, seed) moved ellipsis (...) cleaner function signature. Performance Improvement: internal function .silhouette.simple vectorized optimized reduce computational cost, particularly calculation () b(). Removed fast.calc option nmfkc() function. Added X.init argument nmfkc() function, allowing selection 'kmeans' 'nndsvd' initialization methods. penalty term changed tr(CC') tr(BB') = tr(CAA'C'). Implemented internal .z xnorm functions. Added fast.calc option nmfkc() function. Optimized internal calculations improved performance. Updated citation("nmfkc") added AIC/BIC output. Implemented nmfkc.ar.stationarity() function. Modified z() function. Used crossprod() faster matrix multiplication. Implemented nmfkc.ar.DOT() function. Added logic sort columns X form unit matrix special cases. Implemented nmfkc.kernel.beta.cv() nmfkc.ar.degree.cv() functions. Set default column names X Basis1, Basis2, etc. Added X.prob X.cluster return object. Skipped CPCC silhouette calculations save.time = TRUE. Added prototype nmfkc.ar() function. Added criterion argument nmfkc() function support multiple criteria. Updated nmfkc.rank() function. Added criterion argument nmfkc.rank() function. Implemented save.time argument. Implemented nmfkc.rank() function. Implemented nstart option kmeans() function. Added experimental implementation nmfkc.rank() function. Removed zero-variance columns rows warning. Added source references documentation. Renamed several components clarity: nmfkcreg nmfkc create.kernel nmfkc.kernel nmfkcreg.cv nmfkc.cv P B.prob cluster B.cluster unit X.column trace print.trace dims print.dims Added r.squared argument nmfkcreg.cv() function. nmfkcreg(): Added dims argument check matrix sizes. Added unit argument normalize basis matrix columns. Modified create.kernel() function support prediction. Updated examples GitHub. Removed YHAT return value; use XB instead. Added cluster return value hard clustering.
